steps.title: Identify the rate of change/intercept
steps.content: The slope $3$ is the rate (mph per second) and $40$ is the initial speed.

steps.title: Evaluate at t=5
steps.content: At $t=5$: $$s = 40 + 3(5) = 55 \text{ mph}.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. In the given equation, $s$ is the speed, in miles per hour, of a certain car $t$ seconds after it began to accelerate. Therefore, the speed of the car, in miles per hour, $5$ seconds after it began to accelerate can be found by substituting $5$ for $t$ in the given equation, which yields $s=40+3\left(5\right)$, or $s=55$. Thus, the speed of the car $5$ seconds after it began to accelerate is $55$ miles per hour.

steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
